hardware.description: Dell PowerEdge R670 - 1U Rack Server with Intel Xeon 6700E (Granite Rapids)

deployment_notes: Used as Kubernetes control plane nodes
Managed via iDRAC integrated Dell Remote Access Controller
Supports Dell OpenManage and Redfish APIs

related_variants.difference: 2U form factor with more expansion slots and drive bays

known_issues: PCIe errors at bus 99:02.0 and 100:00.0 on some batch units (NIC/riser related)
PCIe errors at bus 99:02.0 and 100:00.0 may persist on affected batch units (NIC/riser related)

changes: Latest iDRAC firmware for PowerEdge R670

boss.description: Dell BOSS (Boot Optimized Storage Solution) controller firmware

research_notes.doc_discovery_method: BMC Redfish queries + Dell support portal
notes: 16th generation Dell PowerEdge server (R670 = 1U Intel single-socket rack)
CPU-only node with no GPU slots -- used for k8s control plane workloads
iDRAC is Dell's BMC implementation (integrated Dell Remote Access Controller)
Known PCIe bus errors at 99:02.0 and 100:00.0 on batch units are NIC/riser related
Intel Xeon 6700E = efficiency cores variant of Granite Rapids platform

known_configurations.name: Kubernetes Control Plane
known_configurations.notes: Deployed as k8s control plane nodes. CPU-only, no GPU. iDRAC 1.20.80.50.

sol_serial_settings.notes: iDRAC10 SOL requires COM2; racadm 'console com2' is the only valid option

racadm_ssh.notes: SSH to iDRAC for racadm works but is flaky; IPMI SOL fails due to 20-char password limit

grub_recovery.notes: Uses GRUB command-line mode via SOL to boot with systemd.mask= and systemd.run= kernel parameters for self-healing service disable

known_issues.issue: PCIe AER Unsupported Request errors crash Mellanox NIC
known_issues.symptom: BMC Health CRITICAL, PCIe AER errors (PCI3040) on NIC bridge + NIC device, SSH unreachable
known_issues.root_cause: Kernel PCIe resource reallocation triggers Unsupported Request AER errors on Mellanox NIC PCIe bridge

fixes.notes: Prevents kernel from reallocating PCIe resources. Works for both CPU and GPU nodes.
Workaround for CPU-only nodes where toggle-acs was incorrectly applied
